'''''UFC 317: Topuria vs. Oliveira''''' was a [[mixed martial arts]] event produced by the [[Ultimate Fighting Championship]] that took place on June 28, 2025, at [[T-Mobile Arena]] in [[Paradise, Nevada]], part of the [[Las Vegas Valley]], United States.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/02/ufc-317-international-fight-week-las-vegas-june|title=UFC 317 headlines International Fight Week 2025 in late June|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King |date=2025-02-11|access-date=2025-02-11}}</ref>

==Background==
The event was held during the UFC's annual International Fight Week. The 2025 [[UFC Hall of Fame]] induction ceremony also took place during the week.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/2/11/24363543/ufc-reveals-dates-for-ufc-317-international-fight-week-2025 |title=UFC reveals dates for UFC 317, International Fight Week 2025|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2025-02-11|access-date=2025-02-11}}</ref>

A [[UFC Lightweight Championship]] bout for the vacant title between former [[UFC Featherweight Champion]] [[Ilia Topuria]] and former champion [[Charles Oliveira]] served as the event's headliner. Current champion [[Islam Makhachev]] vacated the title in order to challenge for the [[UFC Welterweight Championship]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmamania.com/2025/5/13/24429594/dana-white-drops-ufc-317-fight-ilia-topuria-vs-charles-oliveira-headlines-vacant-lightweight-title|title=Vegas heat! Dana White drops UFC 317 fight card - Ilia Topuria vs. Charles Oliveira headlines for vacant lightweight title|publisher=mmamania.com|author=Alexander Behunin|date=2025-05-13|access-date=2025-05-13}}</ref> Topuria vacated his featherweight title in April in order to move up to lightweight.<ref>{{Cite web|title=UFC 314 headlined by Alexander Volkanovski vs. Diego Lopes title bout with Ilia Topuria vacating|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/02/ufc-314-fight-card-volkanovski-vs-lopes|date=2025-02-19|access-date=2025-02-19|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King}}</ref> [[Arman Tsarukyan]] (who was previously scheduled to challenge for the title at [[UFC 311]] but pulled out a day before the event due to injury) served as backup and potential replacement for this fight.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/6/16/24450393/arman-tsarukyan-backing-up-ufc-317-main-event-between-ilia-topuria-and-charles-oliveira|title=Arman Tsarukyan backing up UFC 317 main event between Ilia Topuria and Charles Oliveira|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Damon Martin|date=2025-06-16|access-date=2025-06-17}}</ref>

A [[UFC Flyweight Championship]] bout between current champion [[Alexandre Pantoja]] and former interim title challenger [[Kai Kara-France]] co-headlined the event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.telecomasia.net/news/mma/source-alexandre-pantoja-to-face-kai-kara-france-on-june-29-at-ufc-317/|title=Source: Alexandre Pantoja to Face Kai Kara-France on June 29 at UFC 317|publisher=telecomasia.net|author=Marina Magomedova|date=2025-04-06|access-date=2025-04-09}}</ref> The bout was originally rumored to take place at [[UFC 314]] and [[UFC 316]], but the bookings were never confirmed.<ref>{{Cite web|date=2025-01-29|title=Report: Alexandre Pantoja vs. Kai Kara-France set for UFC 314 in Miami on April 12|url=https://www.mmanews.com/news/ufc/alexandre-pantoja-vs-kara-france-ufc-314/|access-date=2025-01-29|publisher=mmanews.com|author=Harvey Leonard}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://superfight.net/noticias/306739/alexandre-pantoja-encarar-antiga-vitima-ufc-316/|title=Alexandre Pantoja in talks to defend belt against former victim at UFC 316|publisher=superfight.net|author=Leo Guimarães|date=2025-02-24|access-date=2025-02-25}}</ref> The pairing previously met in an exhibition match in the quarterfinals of [[The Ultimate Fighter: Tournament of Champions]] in November 2016, when Pantoja won the two-round bout by unanimous decision.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2016/11/episode-no-8-recap-the-ultimate-fighter-24-tournament-of-champions|title=Episode No. 8 recap: 'The Ultimate Fighter 24: Tournament of Champions'|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Staff|date=2016-11-02|access-date=2025-02-15}}</ref>

A flyweight bout between former title challenger (also former [[Legacy_Fighting_Alliance#LFA_Flyweight_Championship|LFA Flyweight Champion]]) [[Brandon Royval]] and former [[Rizin Fighting Federation#Bantamweight Championship|RIZIN Bantamweight Champion]] [[Manel Kape]] was scheduled to take part at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/04/ufc-317-manel-kape-vs-brandon-royval-rebooked|title=Manel Kape vs. Brandon Royval booked for UFC 317|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Danny Segura and Mike Bohn|date=2025-04-03|access-date=2025-04-03}}</ref> They were originally scheduled to headline [[UFC Fight Night: Kape vs. Almabayev]] in a title eliminator, but Royval had to withdraw due to multiple concussions.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/2025/02/ufc-news-brandon-royval-vs-manel-kape-canceled|title=UFC Fight Night 253 main event canceled: Brandon Royval withdraws from Manel Kape fight|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-02-05|access-date=2025-02-05}}</ref> In turn, Kape pulled out this time on June 9 due to a broken foot and was replaced by [[Joshua Van]].<ref name="VanIn">{{Cite web|author=Damon Martin|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/6/9/24446192/manel-kape-out-joshua-van-expected-to-face-brandon-royval-at-ufc-317|title=Manel Kape out, Joshua Van expected to face Brandon Royval at UFC 317|publisher=mmafighting.com|date=2025-06-09|access-date=2025-06-09}}</ref>

A lightweight bout between [[Beneil Dariush]] and former title challenger [[Renato Moicano]] took place at this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/05/13/ufc-317-beneil-dariush-vs-renato-moicano-rebooking/83587874007/|title=Beneil Dariush vs. Renato Moicano rebooked for UFC 317|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-05-13|access-date=2025-05-13}}</ref> They were initially supposed to compete at [[UFC 311]] but Moicano ended up challenging [[Islam Makhachev]] for the title in the main event on short notice.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/1/17/24346063/arman-tsarukyan-out-of-ufc-311-main-event-with-islam-makahchev|title=Arman Tsarukyan out of UFC 311 main event with Islam Makahchev|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Mike Heck|date=2025-01-17|access-date=2025-01-17}}</ref>

A heavyweight bout between [[Justin Tafa]] and [[Jhonata Diniz]] was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.mmafighting.com/2025/4/7/24403291/ufc-317-heavyweight-knockout-artists-clash-on-international-fight-week|title=UFC 317: Heavyweight knockout artists clash on International Fight Week|publisher=mmafighting.com|author=Guilherme Cruz|date=2025-04-07|access-date=2025-04-07}}</ref> However, Tafa withdrew from the fight for unknown reasons and was replaced by promotional newcomer Alvin Hines.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.si.com/fannation/mma/news/ufc-317-justin-tafa-withdraws-main-card|title=UFC 317 reportedly receives major main card change|publisher=si.com|author=Mathew Riddle|date=2025-06-14|access-date=2025-06-14}}</ref>

A middleweight bout between former [[UFC Middleweight Championship]] challenger [[Paulo Costa]] and [[Roman Kopylov]] was scheduled for this event.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://agfight.com/ufc/pressionado-paulo-borrachinha-volta-ao-octogono-contra-roman-kopylov-no-ufc-317/#google_vignette|title=Under pressure, Paulo Borrachinha returns to the octagon against Roman Kopylov at UFC 317|publisher=agfight.com|author=Geovanne Peçanha|date=2025-05-14|access-date=2025-06-17}} {{in lang|pt-br}}</ref> However, for unknown reasons the bout was moved to [[UFC 318]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.lowkickmma.com/paulo-costa-fight-roman-kopylov-moved-ufc-318/|title=Report – Paulo Costa return fight with Roman Kopylov moved to UFC 318 in New Orleans|publisher=lowkickmma.com|author=Ross Markey|date=2025-06-17|access-date=2025-06-17}}</ref>

Sedriques Dumas was scheduled to face undefeated promotional newcomer Jackson McVey at this event in a middleweight bout.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://cagesidepress.com/2025/06/20/despite-recent-criminal-charges-sedriques-dumas-books-fight-ufc-317/|title=Despite Recent Criminal Charges, Sedriques Dumas Books Fight at UFC 317|publisher=cagesidepress.com|author=Staff|date=2025-06-20|access-date=2025-06-25}}</ref> However, Dumas pulled out for unknown reasons and was replaced by another undefeated newcomer Christopher Ewert.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://mmajunkie.usatoday.com/story/sports/ufc/2025/06/24/ufc-317-sedriques-dumas-christopher-ewert-jackson-mcvey/84343710007/|title=Christopher Ewert steps in to take on Jackson McVey|publisher=mmajunkie.usatoday.com|author=Nolan King|date=2025-06-24|access-date=2025-06-24}}</ref> Ewert then proceeded to miss weight by 10 pounds and was cut the same day, forcing the fight to be cancelled.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://bloodyelbow.com/2025/06/27/ufc-317-star-instantly-fired-after-coming-in-10lbs-overweight-for-international-fight-week-clash/|title=UFC 317 star instantly fired after coming in 10 lbs overweight for International Fight Week clash|publisher=bloodyelbow.com|author=Jordan Ellis|date=2025-06-27|access-date=2025-06-27}}</ref>

== Bonus awards ==
The following fighters received $50,000 bonuses.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.bjpenn.com/mma-news/ufc-317/ufc-317-bonus-report-joshua-van-and-brandon-royval-earn-fotn-honors/|title=UFC 317 Bonus Report: Joshua Van and Brandon Royval earn FOTN honors|publisher=bjpenn.com|author=Chris Taylor|date=2025-06-28|access-date=2025-06-28}}</ref>
*'''Fight of the Night: Joshua Van vs. Brandon Royval'''
*'''Performance of the Night: Ilia Topuria''' and '''Gregory Rodrigues'''
